title: cistus vulgaris var. albidus
intro: Cistus vulgaris var. albidus, also known as white rockrose, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its white flowers and silvery-gray leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of white rockrose have been associated with several health benefits, including wound healing, antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. White rockrose can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to support skin health and wound healing.
